hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2 TM5/TM6 and reduce off-target affinity. This combination will increase the selectivity margin (Δmargin) by strengthening SSTR2 binding and weakening off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is updated to emphasize the importance of disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to enhance selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5. The focus will shift to testing mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. These positions are non-pharmacophore and can be mutated to reduce off-target binding.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 without compromising the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10)) or disulfide bond (Cys3/Cys14).
